title: Sankey Flow Diagram
description: Data contract for the sankey-flow format: a quantitative, value-weighted flow diagram. Nodes are stacked in vertical columns (stages) laid out left-to-right; links are filled bezier ribbons whose width is proportional to a numeric value (flow-of-funds, request throughput across a pipeline, user-journey volume). It is NOT a free-form topology and NOT a process flowchart — every link carries a numeric VALUE and the ribbon width is proportional to that value. The layout (node x by column, node y by stacking incident flows, ribbon endpoints and bezier control points) is computed in JS from nodes[] + links[]. Node throughput = max(total inflow, total outflow). The sample's #report-data is a valid instance of this schema and is the canonical example. Render: a full-bleed app shell — top toolbar (title, flow-unit chip, min-flow threshold slider, node-group toggle chips, trace-path mode toggle, theme dropdown), a large inline-SVG Sankey, a right detail rail for the selected node (inflows/outflows with value + % of node total, plus total-in / total-out), and a bottom group legend.

meta.description: Shared chrome block carried by every format in the family. Brand-neutral: keep {{ORG}}/{{PROJECT}}/{{AUTHOR}} placeholders unless real values are supplied. 'theme' picks the initial brand palette (one of the 9 presets, Black included).

org.description: Organisation wordmark shown in the toolbar / footer. Placeholder: {{ORG}}.

project.description: Project / system name shown in the title. Placeholder: {{PROJECT}}.

reportType.description: Eyebrow label, e.g. 'Flow of Funds', 'Throughput Map', 'Conversion Funnel'.

title.description: Diagram title shown in the top toolbar.

author.description: Author / team, shown in the print footer. Placeholder: {{AUTHOR}}.

theme.description: Initial brand theme; sets <html data-theme>. Default for this format is 'teal'. 'black' is the mandatory mono/ink preset.

units.description: The unit every link value is expressed in, shown as the toolbar flow-unit chip and appended to every value/tooltip (e.g. 'req/s', '¥M', 'sessions', 'TXN/min'). Values themselves are plain numbers; this string only labels them.

valueFormat.description: Optional display hints for formatting link values. The renderer still keeps the raw number for layout; this only affects what the reader sees.

suffix.description: String shown after the number (e.g. 'M', 'k', '/s'). If omitted, 'units' is used as the suffix.

decimals.description: Fixed decimal places for displayed values. Omit for automatic (integers shown whole, fractions to 1 dp).

groups.description: The semantic groups a node can belong to (e.g. fee revenue, principal, refunds, settlement rail). Each group drives the bottom legend, a toolbar toggle chip (collapse/hide that group's nodes + their links), and the low-opacity tint of links leaving a node of that group.

label.description: Human label shown in the legend and the group-toggle chip.

tone.description: Optional palette role for this group's ribbon tint. 'brand' = the active accent (default); 'risk'/'hold'/'ok' = the three semantic states; 'ink' = neutral grey. NEVER introduce a 4th semantic state colour.

nodes.description: The stages-and-stations of the flow. Each node is a rounded-rect bar in a vertical column; its height is proportional to its throughput = max(total inflow, total outflow). Nodes are assigned to a column (0 = leftmost source stage). 8-22 nodes across 3-6 columns is the sweet spot for one screen.

id.description: Unique node id; referenced by links.source / links.target and the detail rail.

label.description: Node display name shown beside its bar.

column.description: Zero-based stage index (x position). Column 0 is the leftmost source stage; the largest column is the rightmost sink stage. Links should flow from a lower column to a higher one.

group.description: Optional group id (must match a groups[].key). Drives the node accent and the tint of links leaving this node; also the target of group-toggle chips.

note.description: Optional short caption shown under the node label in the detail rail.

links.description: The value-weighted ribbons. Each link carries a numeric VALUE; the ribbon width is proportional to that value. Links connect a source node to a target node (typically in an adjacent, higher column). The renderer stacks each node's incident links to derive ribbon endpoints, then draws a filled cubic bezier between them.

source.description: Source node id (must match a nodes[].id).

target.description: Target node id (must match a nodes[].id).

value.description: Flow magnitude in 'units'. Drives ribbon width and every percentage. Must be > 0.

note.description: Optional caption (route name, channel, instrument) shown in tooltips and the detail rail rows.

legend.description: Optional caption overrides for the bottom legend / help line.

ribbonHint.description: One-line explanation of the ribbon-width encoding shown in the legend strip.
ribbonHint.default: Ribbon width is proportional to flow value.

totalLabel.description: Caption for the grand-total readout (e.g. 'Total throughput').

meta.title: Daily Settlement Flow — Payments Pipeline
meta.subtitle: Where the day's value enters, how it is routed and cleared, and where it lands — by value (¥M)
meta.author: {{AUTHOR}}

legend.ribbonHint: Ribbon width is proportional to settled value (¥M); colour follows the source group.
